Petition For Order Approving Amendment And Correction Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Joseph Yick and Mark Shustoff.

The Court noted that the issue is whether the request for the Parties to sign on to the stipulation to amend/correct the settlement agreement needs to express their representative capacity. Mr. Koplowitz provided history of the settlement agreement, spoke of how it was signed and stated that it was approved, but the Assessor's Office viewed the Court's order as if there were two transfers and that two sets of taxes are being imposed. Mr. Koplowitz also stated that they need certain language to get past that, that all of the Parties approved the stipulation and followed the same format, and that the stipulation was signed in the same manner as the settlement agreement. The Court stated that it will grant the petition to allow the amendment, but it will change language in the order with regard to the settlement relating back to Shew's death and that it is not approving the reading of statutes that it feels are not consistent. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Amendment and Correction of Settlement Agreement (subject to review and modification of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Mr. Campbell stated that Katherine has been appointed as Guardian Ad Litem in the Estate case and that she has the paper work to do it in the Trust. The Court stated that it will review the petition for appointment of Guardian Ad Litem when filed and that it will be granted subject to review of the order. There is no objection by anyone to the Court granting said petition. Mr. Conrad stated that he e-filed waiver of notice. Mr. Freedman stated that Donna has no issue. Mr. Koplowitz stated that Robert signed in both capacities and that part of the agreement is that the probate matter will be dismissed as it is moot. The Court questions signing the order for the probate matter when it is being dismissed. Counsel indicated that they want both orders (one for the Estate and one for the Trust) signed. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release (subject to review of the order). The following matters were heard: PES-16-299442 Estate of Shew Yick and PTR-16-299629 The Robert and Shew Yick Family Trust.

Appearances: Michael Freedman for Donna Yick (Petitioner).
Melissa Sanders for Tiffany Yick.
Daniel Conrad for Robert Yick.
Bruce Methven for Katherine Yick (Guardian Ad Litem for Nicholas Yick).
Edward Koplowitz for Joe Yick.

Mr. Methven stated that they had a number of objections to the proposed settlement, that they made some changes, stated some of the terms of the settlement, spoke of threats to litigate and to remove the Nicholas' Mother, stated that they was a settlement, but when it came back it was substantially different and worse for Nicholas, and that they removed the deadline to refinance. Mr. Koplowitz noted that everything the Court just heard is privileged, stated that two parties are financing this settlement, that they need clear titles in order to do the refinance, that everyone has worked really hard and spent a significant amount of time working on settlement to avoid a trial and that Judge Wiss has offered to do a settlement conference. Mr. Freedman stated that the petition to determine construction of trust can be tried in this department in a matter of hours, that it is a question of power of appointment, asked the Court to consider a motion to severe the petition on construction of trust and stated that the petition to determine validity of trust will be a much lengthy trial. Mr. Methven and Mr. Conrad stated that they would like to have a settlement with Judge Wiss. Mr. Conrad stated that substantial discovery will be needed, and that if Mr. Freedman files a motion to severe, he will oppose it. The Court set a hearing on the motion to severe for August 29, 2018 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. The Court also orders a settlement conference with Judge Wiss. Mr. Koplowitz will prepare an order. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, Petition to Determine Construction of Trust Instrument, etc. and the Petitions to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendments, etc. (2) are also continued to August 29, 2018 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204.

The following matters were heard: PES-16-299442 Estate of Shew Yick and PTR-16-299629 The Robert and Shew Yick Family Trust.

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Joseph Yick.
Michael Freedman for Donna YIck.
Daniel Conrad for Robert Yick.
Peter Myers for Tiffany Yick-Whitney.

The Court asked for an update regarding mediation/settlement. Mr. Conrad stated that the person the Court needs to talk to is a fifth party Katherine Yick who is the Mother and Guardian Ad Litem for Nicky Yick (Minor), that Katherine is represented by Attorney Bruce Methven who is not present and that they attended eight mediations. Mr. Koplowitz also stated that the amount negotiated for the Minor was very generous, that he received a letter from Mr. Methven on April 18th, that he sent a letter back to Mr. Methven explaining what they can and can not do, that the alternative is to go to trial, expressed concern about the Minor's interest not being looked out for and stated that the Minor has no pending petition for relief. Mr. Freedman raised the issue of having the Court appoint a neutral Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor, and spoke of Trust history and Shew Yicks power of appointment. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, Petition to Determine Construction of Trust Instrument, etc. and the Petitions to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendments, etc. (2) are continued to May 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An Order to Appear will be issued to Bruce Methven to appear on May 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear.
